message.content:
You are synchronizing attribute reference names in `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSA-GallopsData.adoc`.

The operations have been renamed from horse-racing vocabulary to boring CLI names. The attribute DEFINITIONS currently use old anchor names with new display text (e.g., `:jjdo_muster: <<jjdo_muster,jjx_list>>`). We need to rename the anchors to match.

## Operation Attribute Renames

For each of these, rename the attribute definition, ALL anchors (`[[old_name]]`), and ALL references (`{old_name}`) throughout the file:

| Old attribute | New attribute |
|---|---|
| jjdo_chalk | jjdo_mark |
| jjdo_draft | jjdo_relocate |
| jjdo_furlough | jjdo_alter |
| jjdo_garland | jjdo_continue |
| jjdo_get_spec | jjdo_get_brief |
| jjdo_muster | jjdo_list |
| jjdo_nominate | jjdo_create |
| jjdo_notch | jjdo_record |
| jjdo_parade | jjdo_show |
| jjdo_rail | jjdo_reorder |
| jjdo_rein | jjdo_log |
| jjdo_restring | jjdo_transfer |
| jjdo_retire | jjdo_archive |
| jjdo_saddle | jjdo_orient |
| jjdo_scout | jjdo_search |
| jjdo_slate | jjdo_enroll |

## Tack Member Rename

| Old | New |
|---|---|
| jjdkm_direction | jjdkm_warrant |

This applies to the attribute definition, the anchor `[[jjdkm_direction]]`, and ALL references `{jjdkm_direction}` throughout the file.

## What NOT to rename

- `{jjda_direction}` — this is a CLI argument (`--direction` flag), NOT the tack member. Leave it alone.
- `{jjdr_wrap}` — this is an internal routine, leave it alone.
- `{jjdo_revise_docket}`, `{jjdo_arm}`, `{jjdo_relabel}`, `{jjdo_drop}`, `{jjdo_validate}`, `{jjdo_get_coronets}`, `{jjdo_landing}` — these are already correct, leave them alone.
- Display text inside `<<anchor,display>>` — keep the display text as-is (e.g., `jjx_list`, `jjx_mark`, etc.)
- Don't touch `// ⟦...⟧` annotations

## Approach

Use the Edit tool with `replace_all: true` for each rename. Each old name is unique enough to not cause false matches. Process one rename at a time to avoid conflicts.

The file is `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSA-GallopsData.adoc`. Read it first, then apply all renames.

## Important Details

Each rename involves THREE things changing in lockstep:
1. Attribute definition: `:jjdo_old:  <<jjdo_old,jjx_new>>` → `:jjdo_new:  <<jjdo_new,jjx_new>>`
2. Anchor: `[[jjdo_old]]` → `[[jjdo_new]]`
3. References: `{jjdo_old}` → `{jjdo_new}`

Since replace_all replaces the STRING `jjdo_old` with `jjdo_new` everywhere, it handles all three at once.

## Wrap Discipline

DO NOT run /jjc-pace-wrap. When done, report what you changed and STOP.

   156→
   157→== Overview
   158→
   159→The
   160→{jjdgr_gallops}
   161→is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
   162→It tracks
   163→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   164→(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
   165→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   166→(discrete actions).
   167→Each
   168→{jjdpr_pace}
   169→maintains a history of
   170→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   171→(plan refinements).
   172→
   173→== Design Principles
   174→
   175→=== Exclusive JSON Ownership
   176→
   177→All access to the
   178→{jjdgr_gallops}
   179→goes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
   180→No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
   181→This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.
   182→
   183→The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
   184→The internet is full of bad bash.
   185→Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
   186→The compiler is tireless; humans are not.
   187→
   188→=== Git as Journal
   189→
   190→The
   191→{jjdgr_gallops}
   192→remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
   193→Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
   194→Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.
   195→
   196→The
   197→{jjdkr_tack}
   198→history within each
   199→{jjdpr_pace}
   200→exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
   201→Git has the full history, but
   202→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   203→let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.
   204→
   205→=== Diff-Friendly Mutations
   206→
   207→New
   208→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   209→prepend to position 0 rather than append.
   210→This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
   211→Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.
   212→
   213→The
   214→{jjdhm_order}
   215→array contains full
   216→{jjdt_coronet_s}.
   217→Each
   218→{jjdt_coronet}
   219→embeds its parent
   220→{jjdt_firemark}
   221→(redundant within a single
   222→{jjdhr_heat}).
   223→This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.
   224→
   225→=== Crash-Safe Architecture
   226→
   227→Bash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
   228→{jjdx_cli} owns all
   229→{jjdgr_gallops}
   230→file access - reading, transforming, and writing.
   231→{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
   232→If {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
   233→
   234→The pattern:
   235→[source,bash]
   236→----
   237→lock_acquire
   238→vvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
   239→lock_release
   240→----
   241→
   242→This keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.
   243→
   244→=== Deterministic Serialization
   245→
   246→Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
   247→For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
   248→This ensures that reordering paces via
   249→{jjdo_rail}
   250→produces minimal diffs — only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.
   251→
   252→=== Identity Encoding
   253→
   254→{jjdt_firemark}
   255→(`₣` prefix) and
   256→{jjdt_coronet}
   257→(`₢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
   258→The base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
   259→Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
   260→
   261→=== Horse Racing Vocabulary
   262→
   263→Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
   264→This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.
   265→
   266→* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
   267→* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
   268→* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
   269→* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `₣`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
   270→* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `₢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
   271→* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
   272→* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)
   273→
   274→=== Presentation Vocabulary
   275→
   276→The CLI and display layer uses distinct terms for certain schema fields to avoid collision with other project vocabulary:
   277→
   278→* *docket* — the presentation term for the {jjdkm_text} field. What the {jjdpr_pace} should accomplish. Used in CLI output labels and slash command descriptions. The JSON schema field remains `text`.
   279→* *warrant* — the presentation term for the {jjdkm_direction} field. Execution guidance for autonomous operation. Used in CLI output labels and slash command descriptions. The JSON schema field remains `direction`.
   280→
   281→These are display/CLI terms, not schema changes. The schema field names (`text`, `direction`) are unchanged and deferred to a future heat.
   282→
   283→== Types
   284→
   285→[[jjdt_firemark]]
   286→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   287→{jjdt_firemark}::
   288→The identity of a
   289→{jjdhr_heat}.
   290→Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `₣AB`).
   291→The 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
   292→Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   293→Input may omit the `₣` prefix; output always includes it.
   294→
   295→[[jjdt_coronet]]
   296→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   297→{jjdt_coronet}::
   298→The identity of a
   299→{jjdpr_pace}.
   300→Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `₢ABCDE`).
   301→Globally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
   302→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   303→encoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
   304→Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   305→Input may omit the `₢` prefix; output always includes it.
   306→+
   307→*Reassignment:* When a
   308→{jjdpr_pace}
   309→is moved between
   310→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   311→via
   312→{jjdo_draft},
   313→the old
   314→{jjdt_coronet}
   315→becomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
   316→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   317→{jjdhm_seed}.
   318→The new
   319→{jjdt_coronet}
   320→embeds the destination
   321→{jjdt_firemark}.
   322→{jjdkr_tack}
   323→history is preserved, with a note recording the original
   324→{jjdt_coronet}.
   325→
   326→[[jjdt_silks]]
   327→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   328→{jjdt_silks}::
   329→A kebab-case display name.
   330→Used for human-readable identification of
   331→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   332→and
   333→{jjdpr_pace_s}.
   334→Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`
   335→
   336→== Records
   337→
   338→=== {jjdgr_gallops}
   339→
   340→[[jjdgr_gallops]]
   341→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   342→{jjdgr_gallops}::
   343→The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
   344→Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
   345→
   346→==== Members
   347→
   348→* [[jjdgm_seed]]
   349→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   350→{jjdgm_seed}
   351→is the next
   352→{jjdt_firemark}
   353→to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
   354→Incremented by
   355→{jjdo_nominate}.
   356→
   357→=== {jjdhr_heat}
   358→
   359→[[jjdhr_heat]]
   360→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   361→{jjdhr_heat}::
   362→A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
   363→Contains zero or more
   364→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   365→representing discrete actions.
   366→Keyed in JSON by
   367→{jjdt_firemark}.
   368→
   369→==== Members
   370→
   371→* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
   372→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   373→{jjdhm_firemark}
   374→is the
   375→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   376→primary key.
   377→Type:
   378→{jjdt_firemark}.
   379→Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   380→
   381→* [[jjdhm_silks]]
   382→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   383→{jjdhm_silks}
   384→is the
   385→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   386→display name.
   387→Type:
   388→{jjdt_silks}.
   389→
   390→* [[jjdhm_created]]
   391→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   392→{jjdhm_created}
   393→records when the
   394→{jjdhr_heat}
   395→was nominated.
   396→Format: YYMMDD.
   397→
   398→* [[jjdhm_status]]
   399→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   400→{jjdhm_status}
   401→indicates the
   402→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   403→lifecycle state.
   404→Values:
   405→{jjdhe_racing},
   406→{jjdhe_stabled},
   407→or
   408→{jjdhe_retired}.
   409→
   410→* [[jjdhm_order]]
   411→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated⟧
   412→{jjdhm_order}
   413→lists
   414→{jjdt_coronet_s}
   415→in execution sequence.
   416→May be reordered via
   417→{jjdo_rail}.
   418→
   419→* [[jjdhm_seed]]
   420→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   421→{jjdhm_seed}
   422→is the next
   423→{jjdt_coronet}
   424→index to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
   425→Incremented by
   426→{jjdo_slate}.
   427→
   428→* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
   429→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   430→{jjdhm_paddock}
   431→is the path to the paddock file containing
   432→{jjdhr_heat}
   433→context and background.
   434→Format: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
   435→{jjdt_firemark}
   436→(without `₣` prefix).
   437→Created by
   438→{jjdo_nominate}.
   439→
   440→==== Status Values
   441→
   442→[[jjdhe_racing]]
   443→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   444→{jjdhe_racing}::
   445→The
   446→{jjdhr_heat}
   447→is actively being worked.
   448→
   449→[[jjdhe_stabled]]
   450→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   451→{jjdhe_stabled}::
   452→The
   453→{jjdhr_heat}
   454→is paused, not actively worked.
   455→
   456→[[jjdhe_retired]]
   457→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   458→{jjdhe_retired}::
   459→The
   460→{jjdhr_heat}
   461→is complete and archived.
   462→
   463→=== {jjdpr_pace}
   464→
   465→[[jjdpr_pace]]
   466→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   467→{jjdpr_pace}::
   468→A discrete action within a
   469→{jjdhr_heat}.
   470→Contains one or more
   471→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   472→representing state snapshots and plan refinements.
   473→Current state is derived from `tacks[0].state`.
   474→Current silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`.
   475→Keyed in JSON by
   476→{jjdt_coronet}.
   477→
   478→==== Members
   479→
   480→* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
   481→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   482→{jjdpm_coronet}
   483→is the
   484→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   485→globally unique identity.
   486→Type:
   487→{jjdt_coronet}.
   488→Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   489→
   490→=== {jjdkr_tack}
   491→
   492→[[jjdkr_tack]]
   493→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   494→{jjdkr_tack}::
   495→A snapshot of
   496→{jjdpr_pace}
   497→state and plan at a point in time.
   498→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   499→form an append-only history capturing both plan refinements and state transitions.
   500→Position 0 is the most recent; older
   501→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   502→follow.
   503→New
   504→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   505→are prepended for cleaner git diffs.
   506→The current
   507→{jjdpr_pace}
   508→state is always
   509→`tacks[0].state`.
   510→
   511→==== Members
   512→
   513→* [[jjdkm_refined]]
   514→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   515→{jjdkm_refined}
   516→records when this
   517→{jjdkr_tack}
   518→was added.
   519→Format: YYMMDD-HHMM.
   520→
   521→* [[jjdkm_state]]
   522→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   523→{jjdkm_state}
   524→indicates the
   525→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   526→progress at this snapshot.
   527→Values:
   528→{jjdpe_rough},
   529→{jjdpe_bridled},
   530→{jjdpe_complete},
   531→or
   532→{jjdpe_abandoned}.
   533→
   534→* [[jjdkm_text]]
   535→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   536→{jjdkm_text}
   537→contains the plan specification.
   538→Non-empty string describing what the
   539→{jjdpr_pace}
   540→should accomplish.
   541→
   542→* [[jjdkm_silks]]
   543→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   544→{jjdkm_silks}
   545→is the
   546→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   547→display name at this point in time.
   548→Type:
   549→{jjdt_silks}.
   550→Enables rename history; current pace silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`.
   551→
   552→* [[jjdkm_commit]]
   553→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   554→{jjdkm_commit}
   555→is the commit SHA at
   556→{jjdkr_tack}
   557→creation.
   558→Format: 7-character hex string.
   559→Captures repo state when plan was written.
   560→Value `0000000` indicates commit was unavailable at creation time.
   561→
   562→* [[jjdkm_direction]]
   563→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_conditional⟧
   564→{jjdkm_direction}
   565→contains execution guidance for autonomous operation.
   566→Required when
   567→{jjdkm_state}
   568→is
   569→{jjdpe_bridled};
   570→forbidden otherwise.
   571→Describes which agent to dispatch, parallelism strategy, or other execution parameters.
   572→
   573→==== State Values
   574→
   575→[[jjdpe_rough]]
   576→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   577→{jjdpe_rough}::
   578→The
   579→{jjdpr_pace}
   580→needs clarification before work can proceed.
   581→When context is assembled for a
   582→{jjdpe_rough}
   583→pace, the model is guided to refine the specification rather than begin execution.
   584→
   585→[[jjdpe_bridled]]
   586→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   587→{jjdpe_bridled}::
   588→The
   589→{jjdpr_pace}
   590→is ready for autonomous execution.
   591→The
   592→{jjdkm_direction}
   593→field contains explicit guidance for the executing agent.
   594→When context is assembled for a
   595→{jjdpe_bridled}
   596→pace, the model proceeds without human confirmation.
   597→
   598→[[jjdpe_complete]]
   599→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   600→{jjdpe_complete}::
   601→The
   602→{jjdpr_pace}
   603→has been successfully finished.
   604→
   605→[[jjdpe_abandoned]]
   606→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   607→{jjdpe_abandoned}::
   608→The
   609→{jjdpr_pace}
   610→was stopped without completion.
   611→
   612→==== State Classification Predicates
   613→
   614→Predicates for querying pace states by semantic category.
   615→
   616→[[jjdpe_defined]] {jjdpe_defined}:: Pace contributes to heat's scope.
   617→* rough: true
   618→* bridled: true
   619→* complete: true
   620→* abandoned: false
   621→
   622→[[jjdpe_resolved]] {jjdpe_resolved}:: Pace requires no further action.
   623→* rough: false
   624→* bridled: false
   625→* complete: true
   626→* abandoned: true
   627→
   628→Future states declare their predicate values when added. Query logic uses predicates, not state names.

   673→== Serialization
   674→
   675→[[jjdz_charset]]
   676→{jjdz_charset}::
   677→The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
   678→Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 §5):
   679→`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
   680→Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
   681→This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
   682→Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.
   683→
   684→[[jjdz_encoding]]
   685→{jjdz_encoding}::
   686→The algorithm for encoding
   687→{jjdt_firemark}
   688→and
   689→{jjdt_coronet}
   690→values.
   691→+
   692→*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
   693→+
   694→. Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters
   695→. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `₣` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
   696→. To decode `₣XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
   697→+
   698→*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
   699→+
   700→. Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters
   701→. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
   702→. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
   703→. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `₢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
   704→. To decode `₢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
   705→+
   706→*Input flexibility:*
   707→+
   708→CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
   709→Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
   710→Output always includes the prefix (`₣` or `₢`).

   926→== Commit Message Architecture
   927→
   928→All JJ commits include an action code that serves multiple purposes:
   929→- **Parsing steeplechase history**: Distinguishes between different operation types
   930→- **Semantic clarity**: Indicates the nature and context of each commit
   931→- **Consistency**: Applies uniformly across pace-level and heat-level operations

  1002→==== {jjdo_revise_docket}
  1003→
  1004→Update a {jjdpr_pace_p} docket text.
  1005→Reads new docket from stdin.
  1006→Carries forward current state and silks.

  1173→
  1174→=== Steeplechase Operations
  1175→
  1176→The steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
  1177→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1178→JSON.
  1179→Commit messages follow patterns that
  1180→{jjdo_rein}
  1181→can parse to reconstruct session history.
  1182→
  1183→==== Commit Message Patterns
  1184→
  1185→All JJ commits use the format: `jjb:HALLMARK:IDENTITY:ACTION: message`
  1186→
  1187→* `jjb` — JJ brand prefix (literal)
  1188→* `HALLMARK` — Version identifier. Format: NNNN (installed) or NNNN-xxxxxxx (dev). Source: `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` (installed) or max hallmark from `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/vovr_registry.json` + git HEAD (Kit Forge)
  1189→* `IDENTITY` — ₢CORONET for pace-level, ₣FIREMARK for heat-level
  1190→* `ACTION` — single-letter code (required for all commits)
  1191→
  1192→*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
  1193→----
  1194→jjb:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:n: commit message
  1195→
  1196→Co-Authored-By: ...
  1197→----
  1198→
  1199→Standard notch commits use `n` to denote routine progress tracking.
  1200→
  1201→*Pace-level chalk* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
  1202→----
  1203→jjb:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:A: description
  1204→
  1205→Co-Authored-By: ...
  1206→----
  1207→
  1208→*Bridle marker* (created when pace transitions to bridled state):
  1209→----
  1210→jjb:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:B: {agent} | {silks}
  1211→
  1212→{full direction text}
  1213→
  1214→Co-Authored-By: ...
  1215→----
  1216→
  1217→Subject format: `{agent} | {silks}` where agent is the designated executor and silks is the pace display name. Body contains the complete direction text that guides autonomous execution.
  1218→
  1219→*Landing marker* (created when spawned agent completes):
  1220→----
  1221→jjb:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:L: {agent} {landed|crashed}
  1222→
  1223→{agent completion report}
  1224→
  1225→Co-Authored-By: ...
  1226→----
  1227→
  1228→Subject format: `{agent} {landed|crashed}` where agent is the executor tier (haiku/sonnet/opus) and landed/crashed indicates success/failure. Body contains the agent's raw completion report.
  1229→
  1230→*Session marker* (created automatically by {jjdo_saddle}):
  1231→----
  1232→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:s: YYMMDD-HHMM session
  1233→
  1234→haiku: claude-3-5-haiku-20241022
  1235→sonnet: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  1236→opus: claude-opus-4-5-20251101
  1237→host: macbook-pro.local
  1238→platform: darwin-arm64
  1239→
  1240→Co-Authored-By: ...
  1241→----
  1242→
  1243→Session markers record when a new work session begins. {jjdo_saddle} creates these automatically when:
  1244→
  1245→* The most recent commit in the steeplechase is more than 1 hour old, OR
  1246→* No commits exist in the steeplechase
  1247→
  1248→The subject contains the session start timestamp in `YYMMDD-HHMM` format. The body contains:
  1249→
  1250→* Model IDs for haiku, sonnet, and opus tiers (full model ID strings)
  1251→* Host identifier (machine name)
  1252→* Platform string (e.g., `darwin-arm64`, `linux-x86_64`)
  1253→
  1254→Model IDs are collected using ephemeral subagents created via `--agents` JSON flag with minimal system prompts ("Report only your exact model ID string. Nothing else."). This approach is token-cheap (~10 token prompt, ~15 token response per probe) and provides accurate version tracking for multi-device workflows.
  1255→
  1256→Pace-level action codes:
  1257→
  1258→* `n` — Notch: standard commit
  1259→* `A` — APPROACH: proposed approach before work begins
  1260→* `B` — BRIDLE: records bridling decision before autonomous execution
  1261→* `F` — FLY: autonomous execution began (bridled pace)
  1262→* `L` — LANDING: records agent return after autonomous execution
  1263→* `W` — WRAP: pace completion summary
  1264→* `d` — discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)
  1265→
  1266→*Heat-level operations*:
  1267→----
  1268→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:N: heat-silks
  1269→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:S: pace-silks
  1270→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:s: YYMMDD-HHMM session
  1271→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:r: reordered
  1272→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:T: pace-silks
  1273→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:D: old-coronet → new-coronet
  1274→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:R: heat-silks
  1275→----
  1276→
  1277→Heat-level action codes:
  1278→
  1279→* `N` — Nominate: create new heat
  1280→* `S` — Slate: add new pace
  1281→* `r` — rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
  1282→* `T` — Tally: add tack to pace
  1283→* `D` — Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
  1284→* `R` — Retire: archive heat (uppercase)
  1285→
  1286→NOTE: Officium markers migrated to VVC INVITATORY (vvb:...:i:), see VOS-VoxObscuraSpec.adoc
  1287→
